OK.

Are you guys making pricing decisions on that or just, just kind of take a pulse for the market or like what does that look like?

That makes sense.
Um OK.
Interesting.
And
OK.
Uh you guys use the revenue management tool for pricing or not?

So it's not at all looking at competitor pricing.
Ok.
Interesting.

So, um we are going out every single night, the tool updates every 24 hours and we are pulling all this data for you directly from your comps website.

So as long as they have a property website with online leasing turned on, you are essentially getting a live data feed within the platform um every single day to see where your units are positioned within that specific comp set as well as we'll track uh concessions daily, whether it's tied to a specific property or a specific unit or anything like that.
I think you may be on mute.

OK.
OK.

So this is your apartment IQ landing page.

So the way it works is you send us a list of your properties and any comps you're tracking for each we build out to shelve the data, sit down with you and make sure everything looks up to par and how it should be.
Um to view the data, you just click view and everything will load up your main reports of navigation for this uh product.
The first one here is gonna be a market survey.
This is your standard market survey unit teams are probably used to.
Um We also have reports for just specifically property level data, same thing for all unit type or floor plan.
And then lastly, uh we do track every single unit within your property and comps set as well.
Um That's probably the strongest report uh in the entire platform.
It's a massive data dump.
But if we stick first with the market survey here, um everything I'm gonna show you is filterable by specific comp or group of comps.
You could also filter it down by the uh bed bath size rent, the date available or that it was built.
And then when it comes to all the metrics that we could show you, you can either show or hide what's most important to you and that is user specific.
So if we start here at this uh property summary across the top here, we're gonna be all the properties in this comp set listed, your subject property will be highlighted here in blue.

Everything is sort from high to low or low to high and some of the metrics that we track from this property standpoint is that the year was built uh distance to your property, total number of units um average rent net effective rent.
We do that calculation for you, the advertised occupancy.
How many units are available, how many are vacant?
And then this is a good way to get a pulse for the market and see who's leasing the most units.
But we'll give you uh that for the last 30 days, seven days and an average over the last four weeks.
And when it comes to the concession details that we track whether it's tied to a specific unit or property, um we can grab that daily for you, whether it's text pop up banner, etcetera.
Uh We can pull all that for you as well.
If we move down here, this will be different pricing and availability for floor plan.
So this comp set has ones, twos and threes.
If you have studios, fours or fives, those will obviously be included.
Um But we'll show you things like the average rent uh per floor plan, any sort of concessions that are tied to it.

So that's for the ones, twos and threes in this cop set.

Um Some information up top here about the property averages across the top as well.
Things like units rent square footage price per square foot days on market.
And down below, we will show you every unit they're advertising is available on their website.
So this is all kind of like a top down approach, uh high level property data.
The farther you get into the platform, the more granular the data will get.
How are you determining um the leases?
